info.title: Backend Connector
info.description: The Backend Connector facilitates real-time data exchange between CoinBridge and your management platform. This connection allows for user validation, real-time transaction authorization, and reporting of financial events.

tags.name: Payment Operations
tags.description: Time-critical endpoints for retrieving user data, balances, and authorizing transactions in real-time.

tags.name: Refunds
tags.description: Endpoints for reporting returned funds and credited amounts to the user's account.

tags.name: Corrections
tags.description: Endpoints for adjusting balances due to technical errors, settlement differences, or expirations.

tags.name: User Events
tags.description: Asynchronous notifications regarding user activity, such as card provisioning or eligibility status updates.

get.description:
### Process Explanation
Used during onboarding to validate a user. The authentication process will fail if your back-end returns `valid: false`. This endpoint is a key part of the user authentication flow to verify eligibility criteria.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the user within the CoinBridge system.

200.description: Successful retrieval of user validation status.

get.description:
### Process Explanation
Retrieves the current available spending balance of a user immediately prior to a transaction. This is triggered when a 'Prepare To Pay' call is initiated by the mobile app.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the user to retrieve the balance for.

post.description:
### Process Explanation
Forwards incoming transaction details to your back-end for real-time authorization. Your system must check the balance and spending rules to approve or decline. This is a synchronous flow limited by card scheme time constraints; failure to respond in time results in an automatic decline.

### Process Explanation
Reports the outcome (approved or declined) of a transaction. If approved, you must charge the transaction amount from the end-user's account. If declined, do not charge the user and display the provided decline reason.

requestBody.description: Outcome of the transaction including status and final amounts.

transactionId.description: Unique identifier for the reported transaction.

post.description:
### Process Explanation
Transmits feedback for refund transactions. Refunds appear as independent transactions with unique IDs and are always 'approved'. You must credit the user's account by the refund amount.

requestBody.description: Details regarding the refund to be applied to the user account.

post.description:
### Process Explanation
Handles adjustments for previously reported transactions. 
- **TRANSACTION_CANCELLED**: Errored at POS after approval; refund the user.
- **SETTLEMENT_DIFF**: Final settlement differs from authorized amount; adjust balance (+/-).
- **TRANSACTION_EXPIRED**: No settlement arrived within time limit; refund the user.
- **SETTLEMENT_POST_TRANSACTION_EXPIRATION**: Settlement arrives after an expiration refund; re-charge the user.

requestBody.description: Correction details specifying the reason and the amount adjustment required.

post.description:
### Process Explanation
Notifies your system about specific user-related triggers. 
- **APPLE_EMAIL_...**: Mandatory email notifications for card provisioning status.
- **ELIGIBILITY_STATUS**: Informs your platform if a user meets criteria to use CoinBridge services.
- **CARD_ISSUED**: Information-only event when a new virtual card is created.

requestBody.description: Event notification payload containing the event type and relevant user data.

eventType.description: The specific type of event being reported.

valid.description: If false, the user is barred from onboarding or using the service.

amount.description: The current net available funds for the user.

validUntil.description: The expiration time for this balance snapshot.

userId.description: Identifier for the user attempting the transaction.

authId.description: Authorization reference ID generated by your system.

approved.description: Whether the transaction is permitted based on balance and rules.

declineReason.description: Mandatory explanation string if approved is set to false.

reportId.description: Unique identifier for this specific refund event.

userId.description: The user whose account should be credited.

originalTransactionId.description: The ID of the purchase transaction this refund is reversing.

refundDetails.description: Financial and merchant details for the refund.

correctionReason.description: The technical or administrative reason for the balance adjustment.

correctionAmount.description: Adjustment value. Positive values credit the user; negative values debit the user.

correctionCurrency.description: ISO currency code of the correction amount.

eventId.description: Your internal tracking ID for the acknowledged event or report.
